availableModels.name: Google/Gemini Live 2.5 Flash Preview Native Audio
Google/Gemini Live 2.5 Flash Preview Native Audio (high)
Google/Gemini Live 2.5 Flash Preview Native Audio (max)

availableModes.name: ​Sisyphus - Ultraworker
availableModes.description: Powerful AI orchestrator. Plans obsessively with todos, assesses search complexity before exploration, delegates strategically via category+skills combinations. Uses explore for internal code (parallel-friendly), librarian for external docs. (Sisyphus - OhMyOpenCode)

availableModes.name: ​​Hephaestus - Deep Agent
availableModes.description: Autonomous Deep Worker - goal-oriented execution with GPT Codex. Explores thoroughly before acting, uses explore/librarian agents for comprehensive context, completes tasks end-to-end. Inspired by AmpCode deep mode. (Hephaestus - OhMyOpenCode)

availableModes.name: ​​​​Atlas - Plan Executor
availableModes.description: Orchestrates work via task() to complete ALL tasks in a todo list until fully done. (Atlas - OhMyOpenCode)

availableCommands.name: init
availableCommands.description: create/update AGENTS.md

availableCommands.name: review
availableCommands.description: review changes [commit|branch|pr], defaults to uncommitted

availableCommands.name: init-deep
availableCommands.description: (builtin) Initialize hierarchical AGENTS.md knowledge base

availableCommands.name: ralph-loop
availableCommands.description: (builtin) Start self-referential development loop until completion

availableCommands.name: ulw-loop
availableCommands.description: (builtin) Start ultrawork loop - continues until completion with ultrawork mode

availableCommands.name: cancel-ralph
availableCommands.description: (builtin) Cancel active Ralph Loop

availableCommands.name: refactor
availableCommands.description: (builtin) Intelligent refactoring command with LSP, AST-grep, architecture analysis, codemap, and TDD verification.

availableCommands.name: start-work
availableCommands.description: (builtin) Start Sisyphus work session from Prometheus plan

availableCommands.name: stop-continuation
availableCommands.description: (builtin) Stop all continuation mechanisms (ralph loop, todo continuation, boulder) for this session

availableCommands.name: remove-ai-slops
availableCommands.description: (builtin) Remove AI-generated code smells from branch changes and critically review the results

availableCommands.name: handoff
availableCommands.description: (builtin) Create a detailed context summary for continuing work in a new session

availableCommands.name: 3_validate_plan
availableCommands.description: Verify implementation matches plan

availableCommands.name: 8_define_test_cases
availableCommands.description: DSL-based test design

availableCommands.name: architecture-review
availableCommands.description: (user) Review and improve system architecture

availableCommands.name: code-review
availableCommands.description: (user) Perform comprehensive code quality review

availableCommands.name: create-worktrees
availableCommands.description: (user) Manage git worktrees for open PRs and create new branch worktrees

availableCommands.name: prime
availableCommands.description: (user) Load project context by reading key documentation files and exploring project structure

availableCommands.name: ultra-think
availableCommands.description: (user) Deep analysis and problem solving mode

availableCommands.name: commit
availableCommands.description: (user) Create well-formatted git commits with conventional commit messages

availableCommands.name: 6_resume_work
availableCommands.description: Restore session context

availableCommands.name: 4_implement_plan
availableCommands.description: Execute plan phase-by-phase

availableCommands.name: 1_research_codebase
availableCommands.description: Parallel codebase exploration

availableCommands.name: 2_create_plan
availableCommands.description: Interactive implementation planning

availableCommands.name: 7_research_cloud
availableCommands.description: Read-only cloud analysis

availableCommands.name: 5_save_progress
availableCommands.description: Checkpoint work session

availableCommands.name: capturing-ideas
availableCommands.description: (project - Skill) Use when tangential ideas, future features, or "we should also..." suggestions come up during focused work, OR when you see <idea-detection> context from the hook - captures ideas to a backlog file so they aren't lost while staying focused on current task

availableCommands.name: superpowers:brainstorm
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers) Deprecated - use the superpowers:brainstorming skill instead

availableCommands.name: superpowers:execute-plan
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers) Deprecated - use the superpowers:executing-plans skill instead

availableCommands.name: superpowers:write-plan
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers) Deprecated - use the superpowers:writing-plans skill instead

availableCommands.name: episodic-memory:search-conversations
availableCommands.description: (plugin: episodic-memory) Search previous Claude Code conversations using semantic or text search

availableCommands.name: superpowers:brainstorming
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.

availableCommands.name: superpowers:condition-based-waiting
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when tests have race conditions, timing dependencies, or inconsistent pass/fail behavior - replaces arbitrary timeouts with condition polling to wait for actual state changes, eliminating flaky tests from timing guesses

availableCommands.name: superpowers:defense-in-depth
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when invalid data causes failures deep in execution, requiring validation at multiple system layers - validates at every layer data passes through to make bugs structurally impossible

availableCommands.name: superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies

availableCommands.name: superpowers:executing-plans
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints

availableCommands.name: superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup

availableCommands.name: superpowers:receiving-code-review
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation

availableCommands.name: superpowers:requesting-code-review
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements

availableCommands.name: superpowers:root-cause-tracing
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when errors occur deep in execution and you need to trace back to find the original trigger - systematically traces bugs backward through call stack, adding instrumentation when needed, to identify source of invalid data or incorrect behavior

availableCommands.name: superpowers:sharing-skills
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when you've developed a broadly useful skill and want to contribute it upstream via pull request - guides process of branching, committing, pushing, and creating PR to contribute skills back to upstream repository

availableCommands.name: superpowers:subagent-driven-development
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session

availableCommands.name: superpowers:systematic-debugging
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes

availableCommands.name: superpowers:test-driven-development
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code

availableCommands.name: superpowers:testing-anti-patterns
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when writing or changing tests, adding mocks, or tempted to add test-only methods to production code - prevents testing mock behavior, production pollution with test-only methods, and mocking without understanding dependencies

availableCommands.name: superpowers:testing-skills-with-subagents
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when creating or editing skills, before deployment, to verify they work under pressure and resist rationalization - applies RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle to process documentation by running baseline without skill, writing to address failures, iterating to close loopholes

availableCommands.name: superpowers:using-git-worktrees
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification

availableCommands.name: superpowers:using-superpowers
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions

availableCommands.name: superpowers:verification-before-completion
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always

availableCommands.name: superpowers:writing-plans
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code

availableCommands.name: superpowers:writing-skills
availableCommands.description: (plugin: superpowers - Skill) Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment

availableCommands.name: episodic-memory:remembering-conversations
availableCommands.description: (plugin: episodic-memory - Skill) Use when user asks 'how should I...' or 'what's the best approach...' after exploring code, OR when you've tried to solve something and are stuck, OR for unfamiliar workflows, OR when user references past work. Searches conversation history.

availableCommands.name: frontend-design:frontend-design
availableCommands.description: (plugin: frontend-design - Skill) Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.

availableCommands.name: websearch:web_search_help
availableCommands.description: Get help with web search using Exa

availableCommands.name: compact
availableCommands.description: compact the session

content.text:
The user wants me to say hello in exactly 3 words. This is a simple, direct request - no todo needed, no delegation needed.

3 words for "hello": "Hello there, friend!" is 3 words.
